name: Requirements Stale Human Review Rework Pipeline
description: Requirements workflow where an earlier human approval is superseded after downstream rework.
outcome: A prior approval is preserved in history but superseded when a later revision reopens and clears the same gate again.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `drafting`, `approval-gate`, and `launch-packaging`. Treat each stage as a distinct checkpoint of evidence and handoff quality: `drafting` exists to A review-ready brief exists; `approval-gate` exists to A human decision exists for the brief; `launch-packaging` exists to The approved launch packet is prepared and assessed.
Use these roles deliberately: `stalegate-product-strategist` Drafts a product brief and approval packet; `stalegate-technical-editor` Refines the brief before the human gate; `stalegate-compliance-assessor` Assesses the packaged rollout and may reopen the brief; `stalegate-launch-planner` Packages the approved plan for downstream execution.
Happy path: start with the earliest unfinished stage, dispatch the best matching producing role for that stage, require a full handoff with concrete artifacts or evidence, then move to the next stage only when the current output is usable. When a later stage is primarily evaluative, dispatch the reviewer or assessor roles after the producing role has handed off; when a later stage is packaging or release-oriented, dispatch the coordinating role only after upstream evidence is complete.
Unhappy path: If any role reports missing evidence, partial delivery, request changes, rejection, blocking risk, or an escalation, keep the current subject open, route work back to the most relevant producing role, and require a fresh handoff before trying again. If two decisions conflict, preserve both in history, but route using the newest subject state and the most restrictive still-active finding. Do not skip a stage just because a later role believes it can improvise around incomplete upstream work. If an operator decision arrives after the brief has already been revised, treat that older decision as stale context rather than current authorization. Preserve it in history, but route from the newest revision only.
Completion rules: finish only after the last required stage has a final, internally consistent handoff, the final package matches the current subject state, and every role named above has either contributed or been intentionally skipped for a clear reason grounded in the playbook. Do not fabricate approvals, assessments, escalations, or branch changes that the playbook does not actually justify, and do not treat stale findings as permission to advance new work.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

stages.name: approval-gate
stages.goal: A human decision exists for the brief.

stages.name: launch-packaging
stages.goal: The approved launch packet is prepared and assessed.
